title: ChatGPT Prompt Mission
description: A spy mission to master asking ChatGPT the right way!

scenes.speech: Prompt Agent! Today's mission is to ask ChatGPT smartly! 🕵️💬
scenes.title: Mission Briefing: Prompt Operation
scenes.content: Your task is to get the answer you really want from ChatGPT. Three success conditions: state the goal in one sentence, specify the audience and format, and produce two versions to compare.

scenes.speech: A prompt is how you 'ask the AI'. The wording changes everything!
scenes.title: What is a Prompt?
scenes.content: A prompt is the instruction you send to the AI. Adding 'who is this for', 'how long', and 'what format' to the same question changes the quality of the answer dramatically.
scenes.highlight:
Things that make a difference:
- Audience (who?)
- Length (how many words?)
- Format (bullets? paragraph?)

scenes.speech: The basics of a good prompt are 5W1H! Decide them in order!
scenes.title: Design with 5W1H
scenes.content: Who, What, Why, When/Where, How — deciding these first leaves no room for the AI to guess. The clearer your inputs, the sharper the output.
scenes.highlight:
5W1H check:
- Who: for elementary school kids
- What: ideas for a free study project
- How: 5 ideas as bullet points

scenes.speech: Asking the AI to 'play a role' totally changes the answer!
scenes.title: Role Prompts
scenes.content: When you say 'You are an elementary school teacher,' the AI matches that voice and audience. Pick a role that fits the goal — teacher, editor, coach, expert.
scenes.highlight:
Role examples:
- Teacher (gentle explanation)
- Editor (cut to the point)
- Coach (encourage me)

scenes.speech: Try sending two prompts on the same topic — short vs. detailed!
scenes.title: Operation: A/B Compare
scenes.content: Compare a short prompt against a detailed one on the same topic. Lay both replies side-by-side and note which one fits the goal better.
scenes.highlight:
Compare:
A: 'Give me free study ideas'
B: 'For 4th graders, doable in 3 days, science-based, list 5 in bullets'

scenes.speech: You don't need a perfect first try! Refine it with follow-ups!
scenes.title: Follow-up Prompts
scenes.content: After the first answer, ask 'shorter please', 'in middle-school words', or 'add three examples'. Each follow-up sharpens the result without starting over.
scenes.highlight:
Follow-up examples:
- Make it simpler
- Add more examples
- Give me the opposite view

scenes.speech: Build your own template! Just paste it in next time!
scenes.title: My Prompt Template
scenes.content: Save the shape you reuse the most. Keep four blanks ready: role, audience, output format, and what to avoid — fill them in and go.
scenes.highlight:
Template slots:
- Role: ___
- Audience: ___
- Output: ___
- Avoid: ___

scenes.speech: Mission complete! Submit a screenshot of your best prompt and earn 480 points! 🎯
scenes.title: Mission Complete!
scenes.content: Passing this mission is less about the AI's answer and more about explaining how the wording made it better. The craft of asking is your weapon.
